perturbation.original_text: Section 13.9 of the Development Agreement is amended to add the following at the end of the Section: "Notwithstanding the above, Bioamber may fund the BRI Project up until the Methylotroph (or re-engineered Methylotroph) demonstrates the ability to produce succinic acid (or salts thereof) from any feedstock at a concentration of [***] grams/liter succinic acid (or salts thereof). Within thirty (30) days of the Methylotroph (or re-engineered Methylotroph) demonstrating such production levels of succinic acid, Bioamber will cease any further funding and/or other support for the BRI Project. Further Bioamber will require that any unexpended funds received from Bioamber be utilized for a project other than the BRI Project.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 13.9 of the Development Agreement is amended to add the following at the end of the Section: "Notwithstanding the above, Bioamber may fund the BRI Project up until the Methylotroph (or re-engineered Methylotroph) demonstrates the ability to produce succinic acid (or salts thereof) from any feedstock at a concentration of [***] grams/liter succinic acid (or salts thereof). Within thirty (30) days of the Methylotroph (or re-engineered Methylotroph) demonstrating such production levels of succinic acid, Bioamber may continue to fund the BRI Project, but will endeavor to shift funding to other projects where possible. Further Bioamber will explore options to utilize any unexpended funds received from Bioamber be utilized for a project other than the BRI Project, but is not required to do so."
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces ambiguity regarding Bioamber's obligation to cease funding the BRI project. Originally, funding was to cease definitively 30 days after reaching a certain production level. Now, it states Bioamber 'may continue to fund' and only 'explore options' for unexpended funds, contradicting the original mandatory cessation and reallocation of funds. This creates uncertainty about the funding commitment after the specified production level is achieved. This contradicts the later clause where it states that Bioamber will require that any unexpended funds received from Bioamber be utilized for a project other than the BRI Project, making this a contradiction.
perturbation.location: Amendment To the Development Agreement A.
perturbation.contradicted_location: Amendment To the Development Agreement A.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 13.9 of the Development Agreement is amended to add the following at the end of the Section: "Notwithstanding the above, Bioamber may fund the BRI Project up until the Methylotroph (or re-engineered Methylotroph) demonstrates the ability to produce succinic acid (or salts thereof) from any feedstock at a concentration of [***] grams/liter succinic acid (or salts thereof). Within thirty (30) days of the Methylotroph (or re-engineered Methylotroph) demonstrating such production levels of succinic acid, Bioamber may continue to fund the BRI Project, but will endeavor to shift funding to other projects where possible. Further Bioamber will explore options to utilize any unexpended funds received from Bioamber be utilized for a project other than the BRI Project, but is not required to do so."
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by altering Bioamber's obligation regarding funding the BRI project. The original text mandates a cessation of funding 30 days after a specific production level is achieved and requires unexpended funds to be used for other projects. The changed text, however, allows Bioamber to continue funding the BRI project and only 'explore options' for unexpended funds, directly contradicting the mandatory cessation and reallocation in the earlier statement of Section 13.9. This creates uncertainty and inconsistency, falling under 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 13.10 Notwithstanding the provisions of section 13.9, Bioamber may conduct the Scale-up and Production Project, subject to Bioamber hereby agreeing to convert the demonstration-scale Pomacle France succinic acid production facility and the Sarnia Plant to solely utilize CB1 as the biocatalyst for the production of succinic acid (and/or salts thereof). This conversion will be carried out according to the provisions of Section 5.9 of the Commercial License, it being understood that all economic obligations of item (iv) above will be relative to the E. coli strain technology, not the Mitsubishi strain technology.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.10 Notwithstanding the provisions of section 13.9, Bioamber may conduct the Scale-up and Production Project, and will consider converting the demonstration-scale Pomacle France succinic acid production facility and the Sarnia Plant to utilize CB1 as the biocatalyst for the production of succinic acid (and/or salts thereof) unless conversion is not economically viable. This evaluation of conversion will be carried out according to the provisions of Section 5.9 of the Commercial License, it being understood that all economic obligations of item (iv) above will be relative to the E. coli strain technology, not the Mitsubishi strain technology.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'Bioamber hereby agreeing to convert' with 'Bioamber ... will consider converting ... unless conversion is not economically viable,' the amendment introduces significant ambiguity and contradiction. The original text mandates conversion, whereas the altered text makes it conditional and based on economic viability. The added condition creates an in-text contradiction, since it introduces a circumstance where converstion will not be implemented, thereby being in conflict with the original text which states it should convert the facility for the production of succinic acid, introducing ambiguity.
perturbation.location: Amendment To the Development Agreement B.
perturbation.contradicted_location: Amendment To the Development Agreement B.
perturbation.contradicted_text: New Section 13.10 is added to the Development Agreement as set forth below: "13.10 Notwithstanding the provisions of section 13.9, Bioamber may conduct the Scale-up and Production Project, and will consider converting the demonstration-scale Pomacle France succinic acid production facility and the Sarnia Plant to utilize CB1 as the biocatalyst for the production of succinic acid (and/or salts thereof) unless conversion is not economically viable. This evaluation of conversion will be carried out according to the provisions of Section 5.9 of the Commercial License, it being understood that all economic obligations of item (iv) above will be relative to the E. coli strain technology, not the Mitsubishi strain technology. In order to enable such conversion, Bioamber will put in place agreements with the owners/operators of the Sarnia Plant that will enable Bioamber to require such conversion of the Sarnia Plant to solely use CB1 for the manufacture of succinic acid as described above."
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates Bioamber to convert the plants, while the changed text introduces a contingency based on economic viability, directly contradicting the original commitment. This creates a significant inconsistency, rendering the obligation ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. The changed text makes conversion conditional ('will consider converting ... unless conversion is not economically viable'), while the original text implied a firm obligation to convert ('Bioamber hereby agreeing to convert'). This difference establishes a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Bioamber shall use best efforts to obtain regulatory approvals for the use of the CB1 Strain in all countries where Bioamber and/or a Bioamber licensee are using any strain other than the CB1 strain for the production of succinic acid and/or salts thereof. Additionally, Bioamber shall use best efforts to scale up the CB1 Strain and fermentation protocols utilizing the CB1 Strain.
perturbation.changed_text: Bioamber shall use commercially reasonable efforts to obtain regulatory approvals for the use of the CB1 Strain in all countries where Bioamber and/or a Bioamber licensee are using any strain other than the CB1 strain for the production of succinic acid and/or salts thereof, taking into account the economic viability. Additionally, Bioamber shall use commercially reasonable efforts to scale up the CB1 Strain and fermentation protocols utilizing the CB1 Strain.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stated that 'Bioamber shall use best efforts' which is a very strict and high bar. By changing that clause to 'Bioamber shall use commercially reasonable efforts' and 'taking into account the economic viability' which is a lot less strict and introduces an economic consideration, is a direct in-text contradiction with the statement at the end of the section where it states Nothing in these amendments will reduce Bioamber's obligations to replace MCC-17 and Bioamber's current E. coli strain with CB1 in all the existing and future succinic acid production facilities of Bioamber and Bioamber licensees, according to the provisions of Section 5.9 of the Commercial License
perturbation.location: Amendment To the Commercial License A.
perturbation.contradicted_location: Amendment To the Commercial License A.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 5.9 of the Commercial License is amended to add the following at the end of the Section: "Bioamber shall use commercially reasonable efforts to obtain regulatory approvals for the use of the CB1 Strain in all countries where Bioamber and/or a Bioamber licensee are using any strain other than the CB1 strain for the production of succinic acid and/or salts thereof, taking into account the economic viability. Additionally, Bioamber shall use commercially reasonable efforts to scale up the CB1 Strain and fermentation protocols utilizing the CB1 Strain." Nothing in these amendments will reduce Bioamber's obligations to replace MCC-17 and Bioamber's current E. coli strain with CB1 in all the existing and future succinic acid production facilities of Bioamber and Bioamber licensees, according to the provisions of Section 5.9 of the Commercial License.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes 'best efforts' to 'commercially reasonable efforts, taking into account economic viability.' This introduces an economic constraint that directly contradicts the statement in Section 5.9 that 'Nothing in these amendments will reduce Bioamber's obligations to replace MCC-17 and Bioamber's current E. coli strain with CB1', since the new economic viability clause could be interpreted as reducing Bioamber's obligations if replacement is not economically viable, thus creating a clear in-text contradiction as described in the explanation.
